summary: Settings page redesign testing - all key features verified. Page loads correctly with new layout: profile card with avatar/name/email/badges, pencil edit button, training profile read-only rows, always-editable injury chips with X buttons, always-editable weakness chips, simplified notifications (4 rows, no descriptions), no Lose It/Nutrition section, iOS-style account rows, app version footer. All modals functional.
critical_code_review_comments: Minor: settings.tsx uses EXPO_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL (line 436) directly via process.env - this is consistent with the existing pattern so not a blocker
No testID attributes on profile card edit button or chip X buttons - adds minor difficulty for automated testing but not a functional issue
context_for_next_testing_agent: Settings page fully redesigned. Profile card with initials avatar, badges (goal/experience/bodyweight), pencil edit button opens bottom sheet modal with name/bodyweight/experience fields. Injuries always editable inline - X buttons always visible, Save injury changes appears when modified. Weaknesses auto-save on change. Notifications: 4 rows (Daily reminder, Recovery week alert, PR alert, Weekly check-in) - no descriptions. No Lose It/Nutrition section. Account: Reset program + Sign out (red). App version footer. No global edit mode banner or floating save bar.
verified_features: ✓ Profile card visible with avatar circle (initials '?'), name 'Athlete', edit pencil button
✓ No global pencil in header - only X close button
✓ Training Profile: Current week, Program start, Training days (read-only rows)
✓ Active Injuries: chips with X remove buttons always visible
✓ Add injury flag link tappable - opens modal with injury list
✓ Weaknesses & Targets: chips with X remove buttons always visible
✓ Add weakness link visible and tappable - opens modal
✓ Preferences: Weight units toggle and Marketing emails toggle
✓ Notifications: 4 rows - Daily reminder, Recovery week alert, PR alert, Weekly check-in
✓ No 'Deload Week Alert' text - says 'Recovery week alert'
✓ No Lose It or Nutrition section
✓ Account section: Reset program (iOS style) and Sign out (red text)
✓ Edit pencil on profile card opens Edit Profile modal with Name/Bodyweight/Experience
✓ Modal has Save button and Cancel link
✓ Tap X on injury chip removes chip and shows 'Save injury changes' button
